title: Undercut and discounted-stop opportunity features from field context
summary: F1 strategy sources emphasize that stop timing depends on undercut viability, traffic, and reduced pit-loss under safety-car/VSC-like conditions.
rationale: F1 strategy sources emphasize that stop timing depends on undercut viability, traffic, and reduced pit-loss under safety-car/VSC-like conditions. Without explicit flag data, the dataset can still proxy these regimes from race-lap distributions and local gaps.
implementation_hint: Engineer race-lap relative features such as percentile of LapTime_Delta within Race-Year-Lap, distance to nearest cars by Position, local spread/dispersion of lap deltas, abnormal fieldwide slowing, and whether the current lap looks like a pit wave. Add interaction features for tyre age x field slowdown x position band to represent cheap-stop or undercut windows.
expected_effect: Captures strategic triggers that static tire-life formulas miss, especially coordinated pit waves and laps where stopping is temporarily cheaper.
risk: Full-race aggregates can leak if not computed fold-safely or if future laps are used. Proxying safety-car effects may be noisy on normal slow laps.
